title: Abstract Vocabulary
description: Nouns and adjectives that describe concepts rather than objects: justice, ambiguity, resilience.
summary_pt_br: Substantivos abstratos: -TY, -TION, -SHIP, -NESS. Descrevem estados, valores, ideias.

scenes.subtitle_en: Nouns and adjectives that describe concepts rather than objects: justice, ambiguity, resilience.
scenes.subtitle_pt: Substantivos abstratos: -TY, -TION, -SHIP, -NESS. Descrevem estados, valores, ideias.

scenes.text_en: Nouns and adjectives that describe concepts rather than objects: justice, ambiguity, resilience. Today's focus: abstract vocabulary. Her resilience through hardship inspired everyone around her.
scenes.text_pt: Substantivos abstratos: -TY, -TION, -SHIP, -NESS. Descrevem estados, valores, ideias. Foco de hoje: abstract vocabulary. Sua resiliência na adversidade inspirou todo mundo.

scenes.title: Abstract Vocabulary
scenes.body_en: Abstract nouns often end in -TY (loyalty), -TION (ambition), -SHIP (friendship), -NESS (kindness). They describe internal states, values, or ideas.
scenes.body_pt: Substantivos abstratos: -TY, -TION, -SHIP, -NESS. Descrevem estados, valores, ideias.

examples.en: Her resilience through hardship inspired everyone around her.
examples.pt: Sua resiliência na adversidade inspirou todo mundo.

examples.en: Ambiguity is uncomfortable, but often necessary in leadership.
examples.pt: A ambiguidade incomoda, mas é necessária na liderança.

examples.en: Justice without mercy can become cruelty.
examples.pt: Justiça sem misericórdia pode virar crueldade.

exercise.question: Complete: "Her ___ through hardship inspired everyone around her."
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "Her resilience through hardship inspired everyone around her."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Sua resiliência na adversidade inspirou todo mundo.

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: Ambiguity is uncomfortable, but often necessary in leadership.
I haven't heard anything about that.
exercise.explanation: The correct sentence is: "Ambiguity is uncomfortable, but often necessary in leadership."
exercise.hint_pt_br: A ambiguidade incomoda, mas é necessária na liderança.

exercise.explanation: Five key terms from the lesson. Match each English word to its Portuguese meaning.

scenes.title: Abstract Vocabulary — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre abstract vocabulary. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Name one abstract quality you admire in leaders.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: What's the opposite of resilience in that context?

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Can you define ambiguity without examples?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: It's the state of having more than one possible meaning.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Good. Now use integrity in a sentence.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: His integrity remained intact despite the pressure.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: True vulnerability builds trust faster than strength.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Abstract words are bridges between feeling and thought.

scenes.title: A conversation about leadership
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, detalhes e sua opinião. O professor vai ler e comentar.
scenes.prompt_en: Describe the qualities discussed. Use at least three abstract nouns from the vocabulary list.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Resumo curto com quem falou, o que disseram e sua opinião.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Over a long Sunday coffee, Zara and her colleague debated which virtues shape real leadership.

paragraphs.speaker: Zara
paragraphs.en: Resilience is overrated; I think integrity holds people together in ways resilience never will.

paragraphs.speaker: Colleague
paragraphs.en: But vulnerability, done right, creates more loyalty than perfection ever could.

paragraphs.speaker: Zara
paragraphs.en: Justice and mercy, balanced together, are the rarest quality in any executive I've ever met.

paragraphs.speaker: Colleague
paragraphs.en: Leadership may just be the disciplined art of holding five abstract ideas together under pressure.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Five words, one long afternoon, and a reminder that abstractions are where adults learn to think.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Abstract Vocabulary". Nouns and adjectives that describe concepts rather than objects: justice, ambiguity, resilience. Her resilience through hardship inspired everyone around her. Ambiguity is uncomfortable, but often necessary in leadership. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: Prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Abstract Vocabulary". I liked it because it was clear and useful. See you next time!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule from last year

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para continuar praticando. Salve os links e volte sempre.
